content:
Issue: SSL termination issues

Symptoms:
- Component: LOAD_BALANCER
- Issue type: hardware
- Degraded performance
- Random devices affected

Root Cause:
SSL certificate expired

Diagnostic Steps:
1. Check service status
2. Check firewall rules
3. Check routing

Solution:
Update health check to correct endpoint

Steps to Resolve:
1. Take screenshots
2. Update health check to correct endpoint
3. Test connectivity
4. Verify logs
5. Notify users

Verification:
- curl https://example.com
- Check latency < 50ms

Prevention:
- Set up alerts
- Review quarterly

Status: Resolved
Resolution Time: 1 hour

Issue: Slow WiFi performance

Symptoms:
- Component: WIFI
- Issue type: connectivity
- Complete failure
- Random devices affected

Root Cause:
Channel congestion (too many APs on same channel)

Diagnostic Steps:
1. Verify configuration
2. Review logs
3. Verify DNS resolution

Solution:
Use WPA2 instead of WPA3

Steps to Resolve:
1. Take screenshots
2. Use WPA2 instead of WPA3
3. Verify resolution
4. Check metrics
5. Update runbook

Verification:
- ping 8.8.8.8
- Verify throughput

Prevention:
- Set up alerts
- Implement redundancy

Status: Resolved
Resolution Time: 2 hours

Issue: WiFi connected but no internet

Symptoms:
- Component: ROUTER
- Issue type: security
- Degraded performance
- All devices affected

Root Cause:
Router firmware crash

Diagnostic Steps:
1. Test connectivity
2. Test from different device
3. Run network diagnostics

Solution:
Contact ISP for connection issues

Steps to Resolve:
1. Document current state
2. Contact ISP for connection issues
3. Verify resolution
4. Verify logs
5. Notify users

Verification:
- traceroute google.com
- Check latency < 50ms

Prevention:
- Monitor network metrics
- Keep firmware updated

Status: Resolved
Resolution Time: 1 hour

Issue: Slow DNS resolution

Symptoms:
- Component: DNS
- Issue type: performance
- Complete failure
- Random devices affected

Root Cause:
DNSSEC chain broken

Diagnostic Steps:
1. Check service status
2. Check firewall rules
3. Verify DNS resolution

Solution:
Update /etc/resolv.conf with valid nameservers (8.8.8.8, 1.1.1.1)

Steps to Resolve:
1. Take screenshots
2. Update /etc/resolv.conf with valid nameservers (8.8.8.8, 1.1.1.1)
3. Test connectivity
4. Verify logs
5. Notify users

Verification:
- traceroute google.com
- Check latency < 50ms

Prevention:
- Regular health checks
- Review quarterly

Status: Resolved
Resolution Time: 1 hour

Issue: WiFi signal weak or dropping

Symptoms:
- Component: WIFI
- Issue type: security
- Degraded performance
- Random devices affected

Root Cause:
Interference from 2.4GHz devices (microwave, Bluetooth)

Diagnostic Steps:
1. Check service status
2. Check firewall rules
3. Verify DNS resolution

Solution:
Switch to 5GHz band to avoid interference

Steps to Resolve:
1. Take screenshots
2. Switch to 5GHz band to avoid interference
3. Test connectivity
4. Check metrics
5. Document solution

Verification:
- nslookup google.com
- Test from multiple clients

Prevention:
- Monitor network metrics
- Implement redundancy

Status: Resolved
Resolution Time: 2 hours

Issue: Backend servers marked down

Symptoms:
- Component: LOAD_BALANCER
- Issue type: connectivity
- Degraded performance
- All devices affected

Root Cause:
Load balancing algorithm misconfigured

Diagnostic Steps:
1. Test connectivity
2. Review logs
3. Run network diagnostics

Solution:
Change algorithm to round-robin or least-connections

Steps to Resolve:
1. Backup configuration
2. Change algorithm to round-robin or least-connections
3. Verify resolution
4. Monitor for issues
5. Notify users

Verification:
- ping 8.8.8.8

- Set up alerts
- Document procedures

Issue: Firewall rules not persisting

Symptoms:
- Component: FIREWALL
- Issue type: security
- Degraded performance
- Random devices affected

Root Cause:
Port forwarding rule incorrect

Diagnostic Steps:
1. Test connectivity
2. Review logs
3. Verify DNS resolution

Solution:
Add connection tracking: iptables -A INPUT -m state --state ESTABLISHED,RELATED -j ACCEPT

Steps to Resolve:
1. Take screenshots
2. Add connection tracking: iptables -A INPUT -m state --state ESTABLISHED,RELATED -j ACCEPT

- Component: LOAD_BALANCER
- Issue type: performance
- Intermittent issues
- Specific devices affected

Root Cause:
SSL certificate expired

1. Test connectivity
2. Review logs
3. Run network diagnostics

Solution:
Renew SSL certificate

1. Take screenshots
2. Renew SSL certificate
3. Test connectivity
4. Verify logs
5. Update runbook

Verification:
- traceroute google.com
- Verify throughput

Prevention:
- Set up alerts
- Keep firmware updated

Status: Resolved
Resolution Time: 3 hours

Issue: Proxy bypass not working

Symptoms:
- Component: PROXY
- Issue type: configuration
- Degraded performance
- Specific devices affected

Root Cause:
Proxy service crashed or stopped

Diagnostic Steps:
1. Check service status
2. Test from different device
3. Check routing

Solution:
Clear proxy cache: rm -rf /var/spool/squid/cache/*

Steps to Resolve:
1. Document current state
2. Clear proxy cache: rm -rf /var/spool/squid/cache/*
3. Check performance
4. Check metrics
5. Document solution

Verification:
- nslookup google.com
- Check latency < 50ms

Prevention:
- Automated testing
- Keep firmware updated
